conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

First, let me review the official eligibility criteria so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex. **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just to make sure there are no other factors that might affect your eligibility, I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or is there any involvement from your local council in paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care, and the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information. 

Based on what you've told me, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16), meeting the age requirement
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you, so you're responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility

You meet all the standard requirements for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by contacting HMRC.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with them, which means the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, ensuring there is no duplicate claim (only one person can get Child Benefit for a child). Alex is not in hospital or care for extended periods, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 6-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex who lives with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospital/care placements or council payments) that would affect eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
